name: Inadequacy of existing QA approaches for LLM systems
description: Participants described a systematic failure of existing quality assurance methods — unit tests, BLEU scores, and manual spot-checks — to detect LLM-specific failure modes, particularly hallucinations and robustness degradation.
key_evidence: Unit tests pass but hallucinations go undetected in production (INT-01, INT-05, INT-11)
Benchmark scores (HELM, BLEU) do not correlate with user satisfaction (INT-03, INT-09, INT-10)
Ad hoc quality processes are reactive, triggered by user complaints not proactive monitoring (INT-04, INT-06, INT-12)
significance_for_qalis: Strongest theme by participant consensus. Present in all 14 interviews. Directly motivates QALIS design.
